title: Bali with Kids — The Honest NZ Family Guide (2026)
meta_desc: Bali family holiday guide for NZ families. Real costs, best areas (Ubud vs Seminyak vs Canggu vs Nusa Dua), flight times, and honest advice on what works with kids.
tagline: NZ's favourite overseas family destination — but pick the right area
hero_alt: Bali rice terraces and jungle landscape
flight_from_nz: 8–9 hours (Auckland to Denpasar, direct or via Sydney/Melbourne)
intro: Bali is NZ's most popular family overseas destination for good reason — it's a 9-hour flight, warm year-round, incredibly cheap, and offers something for every age. A villa with a private pool costs less than a budget NZ motel. The key is choosing the right area for your family and avoiding the rainy season.
verdict: Our top pick for NZ families' first overseas trip. Easy, warm, cheap, and with a high density of family activities. Best for ages 2+.

best_destinations.name: Nusa Dua / Tanjung Benoa
best_destinations.tagline: The safest beaches, the best resorts, the most family-friendly area in Bali
best_destinations.why: Nusa Dua has calm, shallow, patrolled beaches — the only beaches in Bali consistently safe for young children. The big resort strip has everything in one place. More expensive than other areas but worth it for families with under-7s.
must_do: Bali Collection shopping centre (surprisingly good for a rainy day)
best_destinations.cost: Budget NZ$200–350/day family of 4 (resort area pricing)

best_destinations.name: Ubud
best_destinations.tagline: Rice terraces, monkey forest, and the cultural heart of Bali
best_destinations.why: Ubud is Bali's cultural hub — temples, traditional dance, rice terraces, and the famous Monkey Forest. It's inland (no beaches) but cooler and greener. Best for kids 6+ who can appreciate the culture. Many excellent villa rentals at great prices.
must_do: Balinese cooking class — kids love it
Goa Gajah elephant cave (historic, easy walk)
Kecak fire dance at Uluwatu (sunset, spectacular for older kids)

best_destinations.name: Canggu
best_destinations.tagline: Relaxed surf village with great cafes — best for teens and older kids
best_destinations.why: Canggu is Bali's coolest neighbourhood — black sand beaches, world-class surf, Instagram-worthy cafes, and a younger vibe. Less suited to very young children (the black sand beach has surf, not calm swimming) but perfect for families with 10+ year olds.
must_do: Echo Beach surf lessons for older kids
La Brisa beach club (family-friendly, stunning pool)

practical_info.currency: Indonesian Rupiah (IDR). NZ$1 ≈ 9,500 IDR (2026). Use ATMs — avoid airport money changers.
practical_info.health: Hep A, typhoid vaccines recommended. Bali belly is real — drink bottled water, eat at recommended spots. Mosquito repellent for evenings.
practical_info.food_safety: Nasi goreng, mie goreng, and satay from reputable warungs (local restaurants) are safe. Avoid buffets. Check reviews for food hygiene.
practical_info.sim_card: Buy Telkomsel or XL at the airport. NZ$15 for 30 days unlimited data.
practical_info.visa: NZ passport holders get 30 days free. Extend to 60 days for NZ$70 at immigration office.
practical_info.transport: Gojek app for cheap motorbike taxis ($1–2 for short trips). Blue Bird taxis are safe and metered. Car hire with driver is excellent value (~NZ$50/day).

budget_breakdown.accommodation: NZ$40–180/night — private pool villas from NZ$80/night outside resort areas
budget_breakdown.food: NZ$25–60/day eating mix of local and tourist restaurants
budget_breakdown.activities: NZ$30–80/day — activities in Bali are roughly half NZ prices
budget_breakdown.transport: NZ$15–40/day using Gojek and occasional car hire

nz_affiliate_note: Fly direct Auckland–Denpasar on Jetstar or Air NZ. Book 3+ months ahead for the best fares. Travel insurance is essential.
